channel.title: From Rocks to Rockets
channel.description: From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group covers the industries building America's future — AI infrastructure, data centers, critical minerals, nuclear energy, advanced manufacturing, robotics, aerospace, and the supply chains connecting all of it. We follow the companies reshoring production, the deals bringing capacity online, and the technologies being built on American soil. For builders, investors, and operators who want to understand where American industry is really headed. The future won't be built in code alone — and it won't be built overseas.
channel.itunes:author: Alera Group

item.title: Your Old Servers Are a Gold Mine — Literally
item.description:
Korea Zinc is in talks with US tech companies to extract rare earth elements from retired data center hardware — and building a $7.4 billion smelter in Tennessee to process it.
From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group — the podcast about the industries building the future. New episodes daily covering AI infrastructure, data centers, critical minerals, nuclear/SMRs, energy, robotics, aerospace, and the geopolitics connecting all of it.
Chapters: 0:00 Cold Open 0:07 Intro 0:28 The Big Reveal — Korea Zinc 1:01 Rare Earths Inside Your Servers 1:45 Korea Zinc's $7.4B Smelter 2:25 Pentagon Critical Mineral Scramble 3:09 US-China Rare Earth Geopolitics 4:18 The Circular Supply Chain 5:25 The Country That Wins 6:09 Outro
This episode was produced with AI-assisted tools including AI-generated voice delivery and visuals. All content, research, and editorial direction by Jason Sher and Shane Smith.

item.title: The Pentagon's Deadline Just Hit. America Still Can't Mine Its Own AI Chips.
item.description: Today the Pentagon's deadline for critical minerals supply proposals expired. The same week, NVIDIA announced $1 trillion in AI chip orders through 2027. Every chip requires minerals America doesn't produce. The gap has never been wider. Hosts Jason Sher and Shane Smith break down the collision between AI demand, mineral supply, and geopolitics. This podcast is AI-generated and produced by Flova AI for Alera Group.

item.title: America's Chip Comeback: Intel, AMD, and the Race to Build Semiconductors on U.S. Soil
item.description:
The most advanced semiconductor ever manufactured on American soil just shipped — from Intel's Fab 52 in Chandler, Arizona. Meanwhile, AMD and Flex are assembling the most powerful AI GPU platforms in Austin, Texas. In this episode, Jason Sher and Shane Smith break down the U.S. semiconductor renaissance — Intel's 18A breakthrough, the $19 billion in CHIPS Act funding backing it, AMD's new GPU assembly line, and why geographic diversity away from Taiwan is now a competitive advantage for the entire data center industry.
Chapters: 0:00 Cold Open 0:08 Intro 0:30 Intel's 18A Breakthrough 1:20 Panther Lake and Fab 52 2:15 AMD and Flex in Austin 3:10 CHIPS Act and the Money Behind It 4:05 The Taiwan Risk 4:55 What This Means for Data Centers 5:40 Outro
From Rocks to Rockets is AI-produced using our cloned voices. The research, the analysis, and the opinions are ours. Alera Group — Emerging Industries.

item.title: Musk's Terafab: The $25 Billion Bet to Build America's Chip Independence
item.description:
Last night Elon Musk formally announced the TERAFAB project -- a joint Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI venture to build the largest private semiconductor facility in American history. Located at the North Campus of Giga Texas in Austin, the Terafab targets over one terawatt of compute per year at 2-nanometer process technology. 80% of output is destined for space, 20% for ground. Jason Sher breaks down the announcement, the $25-45 billion price tag, the AI5 chip, ASML constraints, and what this means for US industrial independence. A solo episode.
Chapters: 0:00 Cold Open 0:10 Intro 0:30 The Announcement 1:30 Why Tesla Needs Its Own Fab 2:45 The Space Angle 3:45 Can They Actually Pull It Off? 5:00 What This Means for Reshoring 6:00 Outro
From Rocks to Rockets is AI-produced using our cloned voices. The research, the analysis, and the opinions are ours. Alera Group -- Emerging Industries.

item.title: Coal Plants Are Becoming Nuclear Reactors. Pennsylvania Just Signed the Deal.
item.description:
X-energy and Talen Energy just signed a letter of intent to deploy multiple XE-100 small modular reactor plants across Pennsylvania and the PJM grid market. The plan: convert existing fossil-fired power stations into nuclear sites. Talen already supplies 1,920 MW of nuclear power to Amazon from Susquehanna. X-energy is developing 11+ GW of new nuclear capacity across US and UK partnerships.
Chapters: 0:00 Intro 0:30 The Deal -- X-energy and Talen Energy 1:30 Talen's Nuclear-to-Data-Center Pipeline 2:30 The Conversion Play -- Fossil to Fission 3:30 XE-100 Reactor Specs and TRISO Fuel 4:15 11 Gigawatts of American Nuclear 5:00 Data Center Demand Across PJM 5:45 The Thesis -- Convert the Grid 6:15 Outro
From Rocks to Rockets is AI-produced using our cloned voices. The research, the analysis, and the opinions are ours. Alera Group -- Emerging Industries.
Website: https://rockstorocket.com Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/76fng02x1yFx79gAF791S2

item.title: Coal Plants Going Nuclear | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Pennsylvania just signed the deal that turns coal plants into nuclear reactors. And the company behind it already powers Amazon's data centers. Full episode available now.

item.title: You Can't Melt This Fuel | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: TRISO particle fuel -- uranium coated in layers of carbon and ceramic. X-energy says you physically cannot have a meltdown with this design. And they can be factory-built. Full episode available now.

item.title: The World's Biggest Robot Maker Just Bet $90 Million on Michigan.
item.description:
FANUC America announced a $90 million investment to build an 840,000 sq ft robot manufacturing facility in Michigan. The world's largest robotics company has invested nearly $300 million in the US since 2019, creating over 700 jobs. With 500,000 manufacturing positions unfilled and 31% of automakers accelerating robotics investment, the machines that build the machines are now being built in America.
Chapters: 0:00 Intro 0:30 FANUC's $90 Million Michigan Bet 1:30 $300 Million Invested Since 2019 2:30 Three Forces: Reshoring, Labor, Physical AI 3:30 The Reshoring Supply Chain Problem 4:15 Every Factory Needs Robots 5:00 FANUC Academy and Workforce Development 5:30 The Thesis -- Machines That Build Machines 5:45 Outro
From Rocks to Rockets is AI-produced using our cloned voices. The research, the analysis, and the opinions are ours. Alera Group -- Emerging Industries.
Website: https://rockstorocket.com Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/76fng02x1yFx79gAF791S2

item.title: Ninety Million Dollar Robot Factory | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: The world's biggest robot maker just dropped ninety million dollars on a factory in Michigan. And it's not making cars. It's making the machines that make everything else. Full episode available now.

item.title: Reshoring the Reshoring Supply Chain | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: The supply chain for reshoring is itself being reshored. When Intel builds a fab in Ohio or TSMC expands in Arizona, they need thousands of precision robots and automation systems. Full episode available now.

item.title: Live From the Show Floor — SAT Show 2026
item.description:
Jason and Shane are at SATShow 2026 in Washington DC — the world's largest satellite conference — where every conversation is shaped by the Iran crisis and the future of space-based connectivity. From Amazon's race to launch 1,600 satellites by July, to the $42 billion debris problem, to Lloyd's of London offering 'safe driver discounts' for satellites, to the SES-Lynk-Omnispace merger creating direct-to-device connectivity for a billion people — this special episode breaks down why the satellite industry is now central to everything From Rocks to Rockets covers.

🌐 rockstorocket.com

From Rocks to Rockets is AI-produced using cloned voice synthesis. The research, analysis, and opinions are those of the hosts. Alera Group — Emerging Industries.

item.title: The War for Your Phone
item.description:
Day Two at the SAT Show in Washington DC. Jason and Shane break down the four-way battle for direct-to-device satellite connectivity — Starlink's 650 D2C satellites vs AST SpaceMobile's basketball-court-sized arrays vs Amazon Leo's FCC deadline crisis vs the SES-Lynk-Omnispace merger creating a multi-orbit D2D powerhouse. Rick Freeman's quiet exit from Amazon, the FCC slamming Kuiper for asking for a 24-month extension while blocking SpaceX, AST's ironic dependence on SpaceX launches, and why nobody has a standard insurance form for constellation concentration risk, regulatory license uncertainty, or the counter-party web where every company is simultaneously customer and competitor.

🌐 rockstorocket.com

From Rocks to Rockets is AI-produced using cloned voice synthesis. The research, analysis, and opinions are those of the hosts. Alera Group — Emerging Industries.

item.title: America Just Became Self-Sufficient in Grid Batteries.
item.description: Eighteen months ago, the US had zero domestic grid battery cell production. Today: 100% self-sufficiency. LG, GM, Samsung, and Ford pivoted idle EV factories to grid-scale storage. 70 GWh of capacity and climbing to 145 GWh. The EV slowdown didn't kill the battery industry — it redirected it toward the single most critical technology for the AI age. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. Website: https://rockstorocket.com | This show is AI-produced using cloned voice synthesis.

item.title: Zero to Self-Sufficient in 18 Months | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: America went from zero domestic grid battery production to 100% self-sufficiency in 18 months. Full episode available now.

item.title: Batteries Are AI Infrastructure | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Grid-scale batteries aren't just an energy play — they're central to the $650B AI data center buildout. Full episode available now.

item.title: The Infrastructure Underneath It All — SAT Show Day 3
item.description: Day Three at the SAT Show in Washington DC. Jason and Shane break down the biggest themes from Wednesday: ST Engineering iDirect's live 5G NR-NTN demo proving hybrid satellite-terrestrial connectivity works, Bridgit Mendler's Northwood Space closing keynote on solving the ground segment bottleneck with mass-producible ground stations backed by a $100M raise and a $49M Space Force contract, the Via Satellite Awards, in-flight connectivity going multi-orbit, and two satellite cybersecurity bills moving through the Senate. Day three's message: the infrastructure underneath the constellation race — 5G standards, ground stations, and cyber hardening — is where the real opportunity is.

item.title: Jeff Bezos Wants to Put Data Centers in Space.
item.description:
Blue Origin filed with the FCC for permission to launch 51,600 satellites into low Earth orbit to serve as orbital data centers for AI computation. Project Sunrise would move AI compute off Earth — powered by solar, cooled by vacuum, no grid connection needed. A companion constellation called TeraWave (5,408 satellites) would beam data back to Earth. The AI boom is so massive it is running out of room on the ground. The next frontier is orbit.
Chapters: 0:00 Intro 0:35 51,600 Satellites 1:10 The AI Buildout Wall 1:44 Community Pushback 2:18 Blue Origin Answer 2:53 Project Sunrise Details 3:27 TeraWave Data Highway 4:02 The Thesis 4:36 Outro
This show is AI-produced using cloned voice synthesis. The research, analysis, and opinions are those of the hosts.
Website: https://rockstorocket.com Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/76fng02x1yFx79gAF791S2

item.title: 51,600 Satellites That Are Data Centers | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Blue Origin just asked the FCC for permission to launch 51,600 orbital data centers. Not broadband. Not comms. AI compute in space. Full episode available now.

item.title: Earth Is Not Big Enough for AI | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: The AI boom is running out of room on Earth. The companies with the rockets are becoming the landlords of the next computing platform. Full episode available now.

item.title: $40 Billion. Ten Reactors. The Biggest Nuclear Bet in American History.
item.description:
The US and Japan committed $40 billion to deploy 10 GE Vernova Hitachi BWRX-300 small modular reactors across Tennessee and Alabama, delivering 3 gigawatts of new nuclear capacity. The deal is part of a $550 billion bilateral trade framework. The primary purpose: powering the AI data center buildout with firm, carbon-free baseload electricity.
Chapters: 0:00 Intro 0:34 $40 Billion Nuclear Bet 1:07 GE Vernova Hitachi SMR 1:41 US-Japan Energy Partnership 2:14 SMRs: Factory-Built Nuclear 2:48 Tennessee and Alabama Sites 3:21 Big Tech Goes Nuclear 3:55 The Thesis: Nuclear Renaissance 4:28 Outro
This show is AI-produced using cloned voice synthesis. The research, analysis, and opinions are those of the hosts.
Website: https://rockstorocket.com Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/76fng02x1yFx79gAF791S2

item.title: $40 Billion for Nuclear Reactors | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: The US and Japan committed $40 billion to build 10 small modular reactors in Tennessee and Alabama. 3 gigawatts of new nuclear power. Full episode available now.

item.title: The Reactors Are Small. The Bet Is Enormous. | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: The nuclear renaissance is not a talking point. It is under construction. Full episode available now.

item.title: Military Bases Are Becoming America's Next Data Centers
item.description: The U.S. Army just conditionally selected Carlyle and CyrusOne — backed by KKR and BlackRock — to build commercial hyperscale data centers on underutilized military land at Fort Bliss, Texas and Dugway Proving Ground, Utah. No cost to taxpayers. Fort Bliss targets initial operating capability by FY2027. The biggest bottleneck in AI infrastructure isn't chips — it's permitted, secured, grid-connected land. Military bases solve all three problems. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. This show is produced with the help of AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: Wall Street Just Got the Keys to Army Bases | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Carlyle and CyrusOne just got selected to build hyperscale data centers on U.S. military bases. Full episode available now.

item.title: 25 Million Acres Nobody Talked About | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: The biggest bottleneck in AI infrastructure isn't chips — it's land. And the DOD controls 25 million acres of it. Full episode available now.

item.title: $3 Trillion. 866 Projects. Only 16% Have Broken Ground.
item.description: America is in the middle of the largest industrial construction wave since World War II. Between January 2025 and March 2026, companies announced $1.4 trillion in planned U.S. manufacturing investment. The megaproject backlog hit $3 trillion -- 866 projects, each over $1 billion. Only 16% have started construction. Every industry building the future is competing for the same concrete, steel, and electricians. The bottleneck has shifted from capital to construction. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. This show is produced with the help of AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: $3 Trillion and Only 16% Started | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Three trillion dollars. 866 megaprojects. Only 16% have broken ground. Full episode available now.

item.title: Every Industry Is Competing for the Same Resources | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: AI, semiconductors, nuclear, batteries, defense -- all competing for the same concrete, steel, and electricians. Full episode available now.

item.title: $19 Billion in 60 Days. The Robots Are Coming Faster Than You Think.
item.description: In January and February 2026, robotics companies raised $19.3 billion. Apptronik closed $520M at a $5B valuation for humanoid robots. Bedrock Robotics raised $270M for autonomous construction. Waabi raised $1B for autonomous trucking and Uber robotaxis. Physical AI is the missing piece for Americas industrial buildout. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. Produced with AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: $19 Billion in 60 Days for Robots | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: $19.3 billion poured into robotics in just two months. Full episode available now.

item.title: $80,000 for a Humanoid Robot | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: When a humanoid robot costs less than one year of salary, the math changes for every manufacturer. Full episode available now.

item.title: $370 Billion. Every Major Drugmaker Is Building Factories in America.
item.description: The largest single-industry reshoring wave in American history is pharmaceuticals. Over the next five years, drugmakers have committed more than $370 billion to U.S. manufacturing. Eli Lilly: $55B. Merck: $70B+. Pfizer: $70B. Roche: $50B. AstraZeneca: $50B. Novartis: $23B. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. Produced with AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: $370 Billion Pharma Reshoring Wave | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Every major drugmaker is building factories in America. $370 billion committed. Full episode available now.

item.title: Not Chips. Pills. The Biggest Reshoring | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: The largest reshoring wave in America is not semiconductors. It is pharmaceuticals. Full episode available now.

item.title: 128 Weeks. That Is How Long It Takes to Get a Transformer.
item.description: Power transformer demand in America has surged 119% since 2019. Lead times exceed 128 weeks. Wood Mackenzie estimates a 30% supply shortfall. More than 40 million distribution transformers are past their service life. Transformers are the hidden chokepoint of the entire American industrial buildout. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. Produced with AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: 128 Weeks for a Transformer | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: 128 weeks to get a large power transformer. Demand up 119%. Supply short 30%. Full episode available now.

item.title: 40 Million Transformers Past Expiration | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Over half of US distribution transformers are past their service life. The supply deficit is 30%. Full episode available now.

item.title: $3.4 Trillion. Americas Water Pipes Are Failing.
item.description: America needs $3.4 trillion over 20 years to fix its water infrastructure. The funding gap is $2 trillion. ASCE grades: C- to D. Federal investment dropped from $25 billion (1981) to $4 billion (2021). IIJA funding expires September 2026. Every data center, chip fab, and pharma plant depends on water. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. Produced with AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: $3.4 Trillion Water Crisis | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Americas water infrastructure is graded C- to D. $3.4 trillion needed. $2 trillion gap. Full episode available now.

item.title: 50-Year-Old Pipes Under New Factories | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: We are building the most advanced factories in the world on top of 50-year-old water pipes. Full episode available now.

item.title: 40 Billion Pounds of Copper. Buried Under Arizona. Just Unlocked.
item.description: Resolution Copper, the largest untapped copper deposit in North America, just cleared its final legal hurdle. 40 billion pounds of copper. 25% of all US demand. Rio Tinto launched a $500 million drilling campaign. Copper is the physical thread that connects every American industrial story. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. Produced with AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: 40 Billion Pounds of Copper Just Unlocked | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: The largest untapped copper deposit in North America just cleared its final legal hurdle. Full episode available now.

item.title: From Rocks to Rockets. Literally. | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Copper connects every American industrial story. Data centers, transformers, missiles, robots. And the biggest deposit just got cleared. Full episode available now.

item.title: China Has 230 Times Americas Shipbuilding Capacity. We Have 8 Shipyards.
item.description: China has 230 times the shipbuilding capacity of the United States. The Navy has zero frigates. The Constellation program was canceled after $3 billion. The new budget requests $65.8 billion but America has only 8 large shipyards. Shipbuilding is the hardest industrial challenge in America. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. Produced with AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: 230 to 1 Shipbuilding Gap | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: China has 230 times the shipbuilding capacity of the US. 474 warships vs 17 added in a decade. Full episode available now.

item.title: A Nuclear Sub Takes a Decade | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: You can build a data center in 18 months. A warship takes a decade. And the yards to build them dont exist yet. Full episode available now.

item.title: 500,000 Workers Short. 41% Retiring. Americas Construction Crisis.
item.description: America needs 500,000 new construction workers in 2026. 41% of the workforce retires by 2031. Data center construction alone eats 85% of the new worker demand. Immigration enforcement has disrupted 28% of firms. Training a skilled tradesperson takes 5-7 years. The biggest constraint on the American buildout is not money. It is people. From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group. Produced with AI tools. Transparency matters. Website: https://rockstorocket.com

item.title: 500,000 Workers Short and Counting | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: 500,000 construction workers needed. 41% retiring by 2031. Full episode available now.

item.title: Data Centers Ate 85% of the Labor Market | From Rocks to Rockets Clip
item.description: Data center construction needs 296,700 workers in 2026 -- 85% of the total new demand. Full episode available now.
